title: ldapjs createClient with tlsOptions.rejectUnauthorized: false — the LDAP directory certificate is not validated (auth MITM)

params.passDetail: The ldapjs client validates the directory server certificate (tlsOptions.rejectUnauthorized is not false) — the LDAPS connection's TLS identity is verified.
params.failDetail: ldapjs createClient is configured with tlsOptions: { rejectUnauthorized: false }, disabling validation of the LDAP directory server's certificate. Since LDAP bind carries user credentials, a network attacker who presents a forged certificate can man-in-the-middle the connection and harvest every username and password that authenticates through it. Remove rejectUnauthorized: false; trust the directory's CA via tlsOptions.ca.
params.absentCallDetail: No ldapjs createClient call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: createClient does not set tlsOptions.rejectUnauthorized; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: tlsOptions: { rejectUnauthorized: false } is pasted to connect to a corporate LDAPS endpoint whose CA isn't in the trust store. It ships and turns the credential-carrying bind channel into an open MITM target.
